ksa_text: Knowledge of data quality management program design, including profiling methodologies, rule authoring standards, quality threshold setting, issue classification and prioritization, escalation path design, and continuous improvement cycles.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of functionality, quality, and security requirements and how these will apply to specific items of supply (i.e., elements and processes).

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Knowledge of functionality, quality, and security requirements and how these will apply to specific items of supply (i.e., elements and processes).
Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA DQ-K-004 (DQ, Knowledge) maps to Geospatial Data Analyst scope as moderate fit. Composite score 0.4506 clears the moderate fit threshold of 0.43. Top framework anchors: eu_ai_act=0.451, nice=0.435, dcwf=0.416. Seniority fit: appropriate; content reflects IC-to-senior-IC execution work within geospatial data analysis, GIS, and location intelligence.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: P2 include upheld. Score 0.4506 for moderate fit domain is consistent with ANL IC-level scope. No internal contradictions identified.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External validation: does KSA DQ-K-004 (DQ, composite 0.4506) appear in published data analyst / BI role profiles and workforce frameworks?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Survey of DCAT-AP, DAMA DMBOK data quality competency framework; DQ awareness is a documented expectation in senior IC analyst roles does not surface this DQ-domain content as a core or common requirement for IC-to-senior-IC data analyst roles. Score 0.4506 may reflect incidental framework overlap. Without direct analytics-domain text confirmation, external ground truth does not support inclusion at this signal level.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: P3 include overturned. Insufficient external evidence for DQ domain content at composite 0.4506 without confirming text signals. Revised to exclude.

decision_rationale: Single adversarial overturn by P4 external ground truth. Final decision: exclude. Composite 0.4506, domain DQ. Medium confidence — one agent challenged and overturned the preliminary assessment.
